clusters.label: Craving traps and multiplies
clusters.desc: Unchecked desire spreads on its own, binds a person like a snare, and must be uprooted rather than fed.

clusters.label: Do each act well
clusters.desc: Treat every action, however small, with seriousness, justice, and goodwill toward others, without needing praise for it.

clusters.label: Lead by moral example
clusters.desc: Good government works through the ruler's own uprightness and kindness extended outward, not through force or fear of punishment.

clusters.label: Hostility reveals the hostile
clusters.desc: When people mock, slander, or attack you for standing by what is right, their opposition exposes their own character rather than discrediting you.

clusters.label: Inner calm draws people
clusters.desc: Settled, gentle, unforced character is its own strength, and others are naturally drawn to rest near it.

clusters.label: Appetite consumes its own reward
clusters.desc: Greed, idleness, and pleasure-seeking leave a person unable to enjoy or keep what they spend their life acquiring.

clusters.label: Ignored warnings become judgment
clusters.desc: People who dismiss a warning and turn from the truth condemn themselves by their own deeds when the reckoning comes.

clusters.label: Good thought becomes good action
clusters.desc: Genuine devotion shows itself as a good mind, true words, and generous work that build a just order in the world.

clusters.label: Deeds prove the faith
clusters.desc: Everyone answers for what they actually do, so sincere trust must show itself in conduct rather than in profession or fair-weather appeals.

clusters.label: One reality behind everything
clusters.desc: All things, gods, and forms arise from and are sustained by a single boundless source that contains them yet is contained by nothing.

clusters.label: Seeing yourself in everything
clusters.desc: Recognizing one and the same life in all beings dissolves ignorance, steadies a person through gain and loss, and is what sets them free.

clusters.label: Wise speech, willing correction
clusters.desc: Wisdom shows itself in restrained, well-timed speech and in gladly accepting rebuke.

clusters.label: Count the cost first
clusters.desc: Weigh honestly what an undertaking demands and what you can bear before committing, since courage outruns capacity and results take time.

clusters.label: Value what cannot be seized
clusters.desc: Grasping, display, and accumulation destroy what they reach for, while character, understanding, and restraint hold their worth.

clusters.label: Reputation is worth nothing
clusters.desc: What matters is acting as your nature requires now, not the approval of people who cannot themselves live well.

clusters.label: What makes a good person
clusters.desc: Genuine character shows in steady, sincere, upright conduct that earns respect anywhere and over a lifetime rather than in bursts.

clusters.label: Wrongdoing harms the wrongdoer
clusters.desc: Harm done to others rebounds on the person who does it, corrupting them and leaving inescapable regret and suffering.

clusters.label: Losing the self reveals reality
clusters.desc: When a person empties the mind of self-consciousness and stops clinging to body and appearances, they perceive an undying reality that frees them from death and from the pull of pleasure and pain.

clusters.label: Real knowing surpasses claiming knowledge
clusters.desc: True understanding comes through inner insight rather than accumulation or argument, and those who claim to know it do not while those who sense its depth do.

clusters.label: Good deeds bring lasting reward
clusters.desc: Right action and right belief produce a good that endures, bringing peace and prosperity now and lasting joy afterward.

clusters.label: Live by your rational nature
clusters.desc: Do your own work according to reason, accept whatever the universe assigns, and treat all people as limbs of one body, since that is what human nature requires.

clusters.label: Handling fools and angry power
clusters.desc: Fools, hot tempers, and abusive power cause harm that cannot be argued away, so respond with composure, restraint, and generosity rather than confrontation.

clusters.label: Understand things as they are
clusters.desc: Examine each thing and each person for what they actually are — their substance, purpose, limits, and beliefs — rather than reacting to appearances or accepting common meanings.

clusters.label: Uselessness has hidden value
clusters.desc: What seems useless or too large to matter is preserved and grows great, while usefulness attracts exploitation and early ruin.

clusters.label: Accounting for what's entrusted
clusters.desc: People receive resources and responsibilities suited to their capacity and will be held to account for how they used them.

clusters.label: One reality underlies everything
clusters.desc: A single order, life, or intelligence unifies all beings, so the self is deeper than body and mind and to see that sameness is wisdom.

clusters.label: The good and petty compared
clusters.desc: Character, not talent or success, separates the admirable person, who is steady, modest, and dignified, from the petty one, who is arrogant, flattering, and self-serving.

clusters.label: Learning to become better
clusters.desc: Real study aims at self-improvement and practice rather than reputation, joins old knowledge to new, and requires seriousness, humility, and principled companions.

clusters.label: Truth endures, lies collapse
clusters.desc: Honest speech protects and outlasts, while deceit and false witness eventually destroy those who rely on them.

clusters.label: Inner freedom from desire
clusters.desc: The truly good person is content within, free of craving, anger, and self-love, and remains unmoved by fortune, grief, or delight.

clusters.label: Freedom from all attachment
clusters.desc: True nobility is measured by inner freedom — from anger, craving, pride, and every bond — not by birth or status.

clusters.label: Avoid harming and envying others
clusters.desc: Do not scheme, retaliate, envy, or imitate those who prosper through wrongdoing, but hold steadily to what you know is right.

clusters.label: Wrongdoers destroy themselves
clusters.desc: Greed, arrogance, and the corruption of others rebound on the people who indulge them, blinding and ruining them from within.

clusters.label: Wisdom is worth everything
clusters.desc: Wisdom protects, heals, and enriches the one who pursues it, and should be sought above comfort, wealth, and honor.

clusters.label: Reality exceeds words and concepts
clusters.desc: The ultimate ground of things cannot be named, perceived, or captured in language, and opposites like subject and object only arise from a partial view.

clusters.label: What lasts and what passes
clusters.desc: Bodies, possessions, and entire orders decay, while goodness, moral law, and what binds people to something larger outlast them.

clusters.label: Suffering comes from judgment
clusters.desc: Nothing external can harm you; distress arises only from the opinions your mind accepts about events, and it can withhold them.

clusters.label: Everything constantly transforms
clusters.desc: Nature endlessly reshapes the same matter into new forms, so change and loss are not evils but the way the whole sustains itself.

clusters.label: Death levels everyone equally
clusters.desc: The powerful, famous, and celebrated all die, are forgotten, and dissolve into their components exactly as everyone else does.

clusters.label: No one can be rescued
clusters.desc: On the day of reckoning nothing external — wealth, kin, rank, or cleverness — can save a person; only their own deeds and inner integrity stand.

clusters.label: Nature points to its maker
clusters.desc: The order of sky, earth, rain, and living creatures is evidence of the single power that made and sustains everything, for anyone willing to reflect.

clusters.label: Leaders must first be good
clusters.desc: Legitimate authority rests on the ruler's own cultivation, restraint, and promotion of upright people rather than on force or display.

clusters.label: Degrees of guilt and blame
clusters.desc: Culpability depends on knowledge, intent, and what a person was entrusted with, and wrongdoing rebounds on the one who commits it.

clusters.label: Do not fear death
clusters.desc: Since death is inevitable and unknowable, clinging to life is pointless, and only living well in the time you have is worth caring about.

clusters.label: Wealth tests your character
clusters.desc: Riches deceive, corrupt judgment, and vanish at death, while generosity and honest dealing build a life that lasts.

clusters.label: Love as the highest path
clusters.desc: Love, given wholly and selflessly, is what unites a person with ultimate reality and outweighs knowledge, ritual, and eloquence.

clusters.label: Act without seeking reward
clusters.desc: Duty performed sincerely and without any eye on gain, reputation, or results is the only kind that counts, while outward observance without inner truth is empty.

clusters.label: Wisdom versus foolishness
clusters.desc: The wise take counsel, restrain anger, and act on knowledge, while fools trust themselves, parade their folly, and destroy what they have.

clusters.label: Freedom through releasing craving
clusters.desc: Letting go of desire, fear, pride, and the sense of ownership frees a person from suffering here and now.

clusters.label: Integrity outlasts corruption
clusters.desc: Upright living protects and sustains a person while wickedness eventually destroys the one who practices it.

clusters.label: Your mind cannot be harmed
clusters.desc: Nothing external can prevent you from judging rightly and acting justly, so your happiness rests entirely in your own control.

clusters.label: Meet wrongdoing with understanding
clusters.desc: People who wrong you act from ignorance about what is truly good, so pity and correct them rather than resent them, and check whether you share the same fault.

clusters.label: Excuses forfeit what matters
clusters.desc: People decline the thing of greatest importance by pleading obligations and refusing costs, and in refusing they lose their claim to it.

clusters.label: Pleasure needs careful measure
clusters.desc: Pleasure is not simply good or bad, and true happiness comes from freedom from pain and disturbance rather than from indulging every desire.

clusters.label: Rules for daily conduct
clusters.desc: Fix your character in advance and hold it everywhere by speaking little, refusing flattery, choosing your company carefully, and showing your principles through action rather than argument.

clusters.label: Many paths to the eternal
clusters.desc: The ultimate reality is grasped not by ritual or study but through devotion and inward contemplation, and it can be reached by many different routes.

clusters.label: Living in view of death
clusters.desc: Remembering that life is brief and death natural frees you to spend the present acting justly, bearing what comes, and letting go without struggle.

clusters.label: Pride blocks understanding
clusters.desc: People reject truth because it is unfamiliar or unwelcome or comes from someone they think they know, and their dismissal exposes their own arrogance rather than any flaw in the message.

clusters.label: Without self-mastery you are defenseless
clusters.desc: A person who has not disciplined their impulses is at the mercy of their own cravings and collapses under temptation or hardship.

clusters.label: Acting on what you learn
clusters.desc: Knowledge proves itself in practice, and the one who hears and then does is fundamentally different from the one who merely hears.
